metadata.title: IBANforge — IBAN Validation & BIC/SWIFT Lookup API
metadata.description: IBAN validation, BIC/SWIFT lookup, SEPA compliance, issuer classification, and risk indicators for developers and AI agents. Free during beta.

og.title: IBANforge
og.description: IBAN validation & BIC/SWIFT lookup API with compliance data for AI agents

hero.description: IBAN validation, BIC/SWIFT lookup, SEPA compliance & risk indicators for developers and AI agents

0.title: IBAN Validation
0.description: Full IBAN validation with BBAN structure parsing. Supports 75+ countries with mod97 checksum verification and detailed field breakdown.

1.title: BIC/SWIFT Lookup
1.description: Complete BIC/SWIFT database sourced from GLEIF, enriched with LEI (Legal Entity Identifier) data for financial institution identification.

2.title: Compliance Ready
2.description: SEPA membership, payment schemes (SCT/SDD/SCT_INST), and Verification of Payee (VoP) requirement per country. Built for EU regulatory compliance.

3.title: Issuer Classification
3.description: Identifies if the issuer is a traditional bank, neobank, EMI, or payment institution. Detects virtual IBANs for AML/CFT compliance workflows.

4.title: Risk Indicators
4.description: Composite risk signal per IBAN: issuer type, country risk (FATF-based), test BIC detection, SEPA reachability, and VoP coverage in one object.

5.title: AI Agent Ready
5.description: Native Model Context Protocol support. AI agents can validate IBANs and look up BIC codes directly — no custom tooling required.

summary.payForWhat: You only pay for what you actually call

items.0: No subscription. No API key. Pay per call with USDC.
items.1: Your HTTP client sends a tiny payment with each request — automatically, via standard headers.
items.2: Payments settle on Base (Ethereum L2) — fast, cheap, and verifiable on-chain.
items.3: Free testing available via the /v1/demo endpoint and the /playground.

0.question: What is x402?
0.answer: x402 is an open micropayment protocol that uses HTTP status code 402 (Payment Required). When you call a paid endpoint, the server responds with a 402 containing payment details. Your client automatically sends a signed USDC payment on Base, and the server re-processes your request — all within a single HTTP round-trip.

1.question: How do I pay?
1.answer: Payments are made in USDC on the Base network. Your HTTP client (or SDK) handles it automatically by attaching payment headers to each request. No wallet UI, no manual confirmation — just fractions of a cent deducted per call. Check the docs for integration examples.

2.question: Is there a free tier?
2.answer: Yes. The /v1/demo endpoint returns pre-computed example validations at no cost, perfect for testing your integration. The /playground on this site lets you try live calls for free too. No signup or payment method required to get started.

3.question: What about high volume?
3.answer: The per-call pricing scales linearly with no rate limits. For very high volumes (millions of calls/month), open an issue on our GitHub repository (github.com/cammac-creator/ibanforge) — we can discuss custom pricing or a bulk arrangement that makes more sense for your use case.

cta.heading: Start building for free
cta.description: No credit card, no account. Hit the playground, read the docs, ship when you are ready.

0.title: No API key, no subscription
0.body: x402 micropayments mean you pay per call in USDC — no credit card, no account, no monthly bill. Start using the API in minutes.

1.title: Built for AI agents
1.body: Native MCP (Model Context Protocol) server lets Claude, GPT-4, and other LLM agents call validate_iban and lookup_bic as structured tools — no prompt engineering needed.

2.title: Open source (MIT)
2.body: The full source is on GitHub. Audit the code, self-host for free, or contribute improvements. No vendor lock-in.
